description: Classical scale invariance (conformal symmetry) at tree level: no dimensionful parameters in the Lagrangian. Radiative symmetry breaking via CW mechanism.
required_solution: Explicitly documented and validated by critic-agent. The CW condition beta_lambda > 0 is enforced throughout. This is a foundational model assumption and is internally consistent.

description: Zero Higgs portal coupling (lambda_HPhi = 0). No tree-level mixing between SM Higgs and dark Higgs sectors.
required_solution: Explicitly fixed parameter. Documented as a simplification. If relaxed, Higgs invisible decay constraints (BR_inv < 0.11) would strongly constrain lambda_HPhi. Future work: estimate radiatively generated lambda_HPhi via 2-loop effects (estimated O(10^{-16})).

description: Zero gauge kinetic mixing (epsilon = 0) between U(1)_D and U(1)_Y at all scales. No tree-level coupling between dark photon and SM photon.
required_solution: Explicitly fixed parameter. 6/17 constraints ruled not_applicable solely due to epsilon=0 (beam dump, stellar, SN, direct detection, indirect detection). Future work: compute 2-loop radiatively-induced epsilon.

description: Dark sector thermalised with SM (xi = T_dark/T_SM = 1). Requires dark and visible sectors to share the same temperature history.
required_solution: This is the most critical uncontrolled assumption. Constraint C17 flags that xi=1 is inconsistent with zero portal couplings. Gravitational interaction rate Gamma_grav/H ~ 10^{-51} at T=1 GeV -- completely negligible. Required action: implement coupled Boltzmann equations for DS-SM temperature evolution.

description: Two Dirac fermions (psi_1, psi_2) with charges +1/2 and -1/2 are the minimal anomaly-free fermion content.
required_solution: Explicitly validated by critic-agent. The U(1)_D^3 anomaly cancels with this charge assignment. Solid model-building choice, not an approximation.

description: Scalar loop contributions to the CW potential are neglected (gb_scalar = 0 in backend). Pipeline documentation states these are subleading.
required_solution: Literature confirms scalar loops are subleading for CW regimes (Kierkla et al. 2312.12413). However, the beta_lambda formula omits the scalar self-interaction contribution (10*lambda^2/(48pi^2)). Future work: compare with and without scalar loop contributions.

description: High-temperature expansion of the finite-T effective potential is valid throughout the supercooling regime. Assumes m/T << 1 for all particles at the phase transition temperature.
required_solution: The high-T expansion breaks down for supercooled transitions: benchmark points have m_A'/T_n ~ 50-150, severely violating m/T << 1. Dimensional reduction (3D EFT) is the systematic alternative. Required action: quantify uncertainty by comparing 4D HT to 3D EFT.

description: No Daisy (ring diagram) resummation is included in the thermal effective potential.
required_solution: Daisy resummation captures the leading IR-divergent contributions from the zero-Matsubara mode. Curtin, Meade, Ramani (1808.08849) show errors up to 40% in Tc with standard methods. Required action: implement Arnold-Espinosa or PD resummation.

description: Renormalisation scale fixed to the vev (mu = v). No RG running of couplings is included between the renormalisation scale and the physical temperature scale.
required_solution: Significant uncontrolled approximation for supercooled transitions spanning many orders of magnitude. Sher (PRD 24, 1699, 1981) showed running changes T_n by ~10 orders in extreme cases. Required action: implement RG running with scale mu = max(m_A'(phi), pi*T).

description: Gaussian approximation for the false vacuum fraction. The fraction of space remaining in the false vacuum is computed assuming an exponential nucleation rate that can be approximated by Gaussian integration around the saddle point.
required_solution: Standard approach. Athron, Balazs, Morris (2212.07559) show physical volume criterion matters for strong supercooling. Bias is systematic but small compared to other uncertainties.

description: Single-field approximation for the bounce action. The FOPT is treated as driven by the radial mode chi alone.
required_solution: Standard for models with single scalar vev. Multi-field bounce effects (gauge field fluctuations) could modify the action. Feng & Zhang (2602.14866) provide gauge-independent treatment. Required action: compare with full multi-field bounce action.

description: Eternal inflation condition checked but relies on the bounce action extrapolation to T=0 and the condition S_3(T=0)/T > ~2.
required_solution: The eternal inflation check is performed and viable points are filtered. For extremely supercooled points (alpha ~ 10^24), graceful exit (Ellis, Lewicki, No, 1809.08242) becomes relevant.

description: Percolation temperature T_p computed via the Gaussian approximation of the false vacuum fraction P_f(T_p) = 0.71. Does not use the physical volume criterion or JMAK equation.
required_solution: The physical volume criterion (Athron et al. 2305.02357) is more fundamental for strong supercooling. Required action: check the physical volume criterion for the benchmark points.

description: Bubble wall velocity is assumed to be ultra-relativistic / runaway (v_w ~ 1). The DBF template is calibrated for this regime.
required_solution: Critical uncontrolled assumption. Garcia Garcia et al. (2212.10572) show P_mdp from massive gauge bosons can prevent runaway. This model has such bosons (A'). Required action: compute v_w from friction pressure vs vacuum energy.

description: DBF (dissipative bulk flow) template from Lewicki & Vaskonen (2025, arXiv:2511.15687) used for GW spectrum. Valid for strongly supercooled transitions (alpha >> 1) with ultra-relativistic walls.
required_solution: Template appropriate for supercooled regime but calibration has specific assumptions. For extreme alpha ~ 10^10, Li & Nath (2602.14324) advocate R_* instead of beta/H. Required action: validate against full numerical simulations for the specific alpha values found.

description: Bubble collisions vs sound waves vs turbulence dominance in the GW spectrum. The DBF template assumes the dissipative bulk flow regime where fluid motion is the dominant source.
required_solution: For alpha >> 1 with secluded DS and no radiation, bubble walls could dominate. Required action: compare vacuum-dominated (bubble collision) and fluid-dominated templates for same FOPT parameters.

description: Numerical failure rate: 40.8% (1822/4464) of FOPT scan points failed, mostly at high g_D (>1.5) where the bounce action computation fails.
required_solution: High failure rate at g_D > 1.5 is significant coverage gap. PTArcade posterior is within stable range, but the excluded high-coupling region may contain viable points. Required action: diagnose numerical failures.

description: PTArcade prior choice: Uniform(0.1, 3.0) on g_D, Uniform(0.0001, 0.8) on y_psi, Uniform(-4.0, 1.0) on log10_v. Flat priors in linear space for couplings, flat in log space for v.
required_solution: Prior choice affects posterior inference. y_psi 68% HPDI lower bound not existing (extends to prior boundary) suggests prior-driven projection effect. Required action: rerun with Jeffreys or weakly informative priors; extend y_psi upper bound.

description: PTArcade MCMC convergence: 100k samples total, 7,501 post-burnin samples from a single chain for inference.
required_solution: 7,501 effective samples from single chain may be insufficient, especially for tail quantiles. No R-hat (single chain). No ESS reported. Required action: run 4+ independent chains, compute R-hat < 1.05, verify ESS > 1000.

description: The PTArcade posterior is driven by a 14-bin comparison to the NANOGrav 15-year data. The benchmark scan uses a binary inside/outside bin criterion (score/14) rather than a chi-squared likelihood.
required_solution: Binary score discards spectral shape information. A chi-squared fit with NANOGrav covariance matrix would provide quantitative model comparison. Required action: report chi-squared values alongside binary scores.

description: The y_psi marginal posterior extends to very small values without a well-defined lower bound. The 68% HPDI lower bound does not exist (extends to prior edge).
required_solution: Serious degeneracy: y_psi only affects beta_lambda; for y_psi << g_D, beta_lambda becomes independent of y_psi. PTA data cannot resolve this degeneracy. Required action: reparameterise using (g_D, m_chi/v, v) basis.

description: Dark matter relic abundance has not been computed. The lightest dark sector particle (chi in most of the 1-sigma PTArcade region, m_chi ~ 20-120 MeV) is a stable DM candidate but its relic abundance is unknown.
required_solution: Most critical missing calculation. Overproduction (Omega h^2 > 0.12) would exclude large regions of PTArcade posterior. Required action: implement Boltzmann solver for secluded freeze-out; compute chi chi -> A A cross-section.

description: Dark matter self-interaction cross-section has not been computed. For chi masses ~20-120 MeV with A' mediator masses ~340-940 MeV, the self-interaction could be relevant for small-scale structure.
required_solution: Mass ratio m_A'/m_chi ~ 5-20 implies perturbative regime with potentially interesting self-interactions. Required action: compute transfer cross-section as function of velocity.

description: Perturbative unitarity: g_D < 4*pi is satisfied for the whole viable region. More stringent coupled-channel unitarity bounds have not been checked for this specific mass hierarchy.
required_solution: Simple perturbativity bound is satisfied. More detailed bounds (Araki et al. 2406.17760) need dedicated computation but low priority given small couplings.

description: Delta N_eff constraint is satisfied because all dark sector particles in the PTArcade 1-sigma region have masses > 30 MeV and are non-relativistic at BBN.
required_solution: Valid for PTArcade posterior (v~0.65-1.45 GeV). FOPT benchmark region (v~10-20 MeV) has m_chi ~ 1-2 MeV borderline. PTArcade region is fiducial.

description: BBN constraints on late-decaying particles require the relic abundance of the stable DM candidate (chi) to compute energy injection. Only A' lifetime computed (prompt, safe).
required_solution: A' decay is prompt. But if chi is stable, its energy density at BBN (not decay) is the concern. Required action: compute chi number density after FOPT.

description: BBN/CMB constraint on PT energy injection (Bai-Korwar bound T_* > ~2 MeV for strong PTs). FOPT benchmarks have T_reh < 2 MeV (as low as 0.45 MeV).
required_solution: Bound assumes latent heat reheats SM photons. In secluded model, DS is reheated. But DS energy density contributes to expansion rate. Required action: compute DS energy density at BBN.

description: xi = T_dark/T_SM = 1 is inconsistent with zero portals. Gravitational thermalisation is negligible. The dark sector temperature evolution must be self-consistently computed from the PT reheating.
required_solution: Single most critical unresolved issue. Gamma_grav/H ~ 10^{-51} at T=1 GeV -- negligible. Li & Nath (2602.14324) show omitting xi evolution changes GW predictions by up to 4 orders of magnitude. Required action: model DS as isolated thermal bath.

description: The PTArcade 1-sigma region (v ~ 0.65-1.45 GeV) and the FOPT benchmark region (v ~ 10-20 MeV) are in completely different mass regimes.
required_solution: Two analyses point to different physical regimes. Required action: overlay benchmark 14/14 points and PTArcade posterior on (v, g_D) plane to check consistency.

description: The 1-sigma PTArcade region has all dark sector masses > 30 MeV (non-relativistic at BBN), but this is conditional on the xi=1 assumption.
required_solution: All constraint conclusions (Delta N_eff, BBN, DM relic) are conditional on xi=1. If xi is relaxed, GW parameters change, feeding back into the posterior. Circular dependency.

description: The Coleman-Weinberg mechanism is the sole source of symmetry breaking. No tree-level mass term exists; the one-loop CW potential generates the vacuum expectation value radiatively.
required_solution: Defining feature of the model. Used consistently across all stages. Validated by critic-agent. Controlled assumption.

description: The semianalytic_pipeline backend uses analytic fits for the bounce action at zero and finite temperature, not a direct numerical solution of the bounce equation.
required_solution: Analytic bounce action parameterisation may not be accurate for extreme supercooling. 40.8% failure rate suggests fits break down for g_D > 1.5. Required action: cross-validate against CosmoTransitions.

description: The temperature-dependent effective potential uses only the finite-T renormalisation of the scalar self-coupling; running of gauge and Yukawa couplings with temperature is not included.
required_solution: Coupling running affects the DW approximation and barrier height. Related to A09. Required action: implement one-loop RG running between mu=v and mu~pi*T.

description: The PTArcade analysis assumes a power-law SMBHB foreground characterised by amplitude A_BHB and spectral index gamma_BHB as nuisance parameters. The correlation between SMBHB and DS parameters is marginalised but not reported.
required_solution: DS posterior marginalises over SMBHB foreground. 14-bin benchmark scan does not account for SMBHB at all. Required action: report joint posterior of DS+SMBHB parameters and Bayes factor.

description: The extremely large alpha values in the benchmark points (up to 10^10) may be outside the calibrated range of the DBF template. The template was validated for alpha up to ~10^4.
required_solution: For alpha >> 1, Universe enters vacuum-dominated expansion. Standard Hubble-normalised parameters lose meaning. Required action: check alpha < alpha_max for each point.

description: The FOPT scan grid resolution (4464 points in 3D parameter space) may miss fine structure in the viable parameter region. The PTArcade MCMC explores more finely but has limited chain length.
required_solution: 4464 points over 3D is coarse (16x16x17 spacing). Narrow viable region (supercooling condition creates thin wedge) may be under-sampled. Required action: adaptive scan with higher resolution.

description: The fermion masses from the Yukawa term produce a Dirac mass matrix with degenerate eigenvalues (both psi_1 and psi_2 have the same mass m_psi = y_psi * v / sqrt(2)).
required_solution: Mass degeneracy follows from single Yukawa coupling with pair of opposite-charge fermions. Consequence of model structure. Validated by critic-agent. Controlled.

description: The dark sector has no CP-violating phases: all couplings (g_D, y_psi, lambda_Phi) are real. The Lagrangian is CP-conserving.
required_solution: Explicitly validated by critic-agent. CP-violation is not relevant for GW spectrum. Controlled assumption.
